original_abstract: DeepSeek has introduced its recent model DeepSeek-R1, showing divergence from OpenAI’s ChatGPT, suggesting an open-source alternative to users. This paper analyzes the architecture of DeepSeek-R1, mainly adopting rule-based reinforcement learning (RL) without preliminary supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which has shown better efficiency. By integrating multi-stage training along with cold-start data usage before RL, the model can achieve meaningful performance in reasoning tasks along with reward modeling optimizing training process. DeepSeek shows its strength in technical, reasoning tasks, able to show its decision-making process through open source whereas ChatGPT shows its strength on general tasks and areas requiring creativeness. Despite the groundbreaking developments of both models, there is room for improvement in AI landscape and matters to be handled such as quality of data, black box problems, privacy management, and job displacement. This paper suggests the future of AI, expecting better performance in multi-modal tasks, enhancing its effectiveness in handling larger data sets, enabling users with improved AI landscapes and utility.
humanized_abstract: Author Correction Abstract In this paper, we reveal the details of the DeepSeek-R1 model, a new release model, which is diverged from OpenAI’s ChatGPT and should be a viable open-source choice for users. Our model, DeepSeek, which is based on a new design of rule-based reinforcement learning (RL) without any preliminary supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which has been shown to be more efficient. In this paper, we study the multi-stage training scheme and the cold-start strategy to utilize the cold-start data in RL to gain the effective results not only in the technical tasks like reasoning and reward modeling but also in improving the AI training process. It is well known that DeepSeek shows the strength in the technical tasks with an open source to show its decision-making process. Conversely, ChatGPT shows the strength in general tasks as well as creative work. Despite the impressive recent advances, there are many areas which need to be tackled in the field of AI such as data quality issues, black box problems, privacy, and job replacement. We foresee many future developments in the AI domain where our DeepSeek model and other alike models would lead to not only better multi-modal tasks and deeper and wider learning but also to more useful AI landscape and utilities.
